originalH1: Real business platforms, owned by you. Built in 14 days.
originalSubhead: Day14 builds you a complete branded business platform — marketing site, customer portal, billing, admin app, AI chatbot — in 14 days. Fixed price. You own the code, the domain, the data. Not a SaaS subscription. Not a Squarespace template. A real product, with your name on the repo.
originalBodyFirstParagraph: Jobber rents you a back-office for $99/mo forever. Squarespace rents you a brochure. We build you something that is yours.
brandVoice: Specific numbers (14 days, fixed price, 3 SKUs).
Leads with ownership (your repo, your domain, your data).

variants.h1: Own the whole business platform. In 14 days. At a fixed price.
variants.subhead: Day14 ships you the marketing site, customer portal, billing flow, admin app, and AI chatbot — every line of code is yours when we hand over the repo. No SaaS rent. No nine-month agency runway.
variants.rationale: Tightens the original three-beat tagline into the offer's three sharpest facts (ownership, timeline, fixed price) — each beat clears one objection in order. Body follows with a stack list (proof) and ends on the two anti-positions (differentiation). Best for conversion because it leads with the decision the visitor has to make: own vs. rent.

variants.h1: The whole stack, shipped in two weeks. Your repo. Your domain. Your data.
variants.subhead: One operator, one fixed price, fourteen days. Day14 builds the marketing site, the portal, the billing, the admin app, and the AI chatbot — then hands you the keys, the code, and the deploy button.
variants.rationale: Foregrounds the deliverable (whole stack) + cadence (two weeks), then triples the ownership claim into three concrete nouns the visitor cares about. Subhead mirrors the day14 'one operator / one price / 14 days' cadence Jack uses verbatim in PITCH.vsAgency. Higher conversion bet for visitors who came from a 'build vs. agency' search intent.

variants.h1: Skip the SaaS subscription. Skip the agency timeline. Ship a real business platform in 14 days.
variants.subhead: Day14 turns your business into software at a fixed price. Marketing site, customer portal, billing, admin app, AI chatbot — productized, shipped, and yours. Your name on the repo when we're done.
variants.rationale: Direct anti-position H1 — converts the highest-intent visitors who arrived already comparing Day14 against Jobber / Squarespace / agency bids. Two parallel 'Skip' clauses do the comparison shopping in the headline so the subhead is free to enumerate the deliverable. Best for paid-traffic landings where the comparison is already in the visitor's head.
